ground_truth.title: The 10 best gardening books of 2025
ground_truth.author: Fionnuala Fallon
ground_truth.main_content: As another gardening year comes to an end, here's a look at ten of the best gardening books of 2025. Diary of a Keen Gardener by Mary Keen is the hugely respected British writer's latest book about the story of a year's turning in her own plot in Somerset, insightful, instructive, inspiring, and filled with pearls of wisdom born of a lifetime's expert, thoughtful gardening. Wonderlands: British Garden Designers at Home by Clare Coulson explores how 18 of the UK's most successful designers have approached their own private gardens, featuring designers like Dan Pearson, Tom Stuart-Smith and Arne Maynard. The New Romantic Garden by Jo Thompson offers readers a fascinating and instructive insight into her work as an award-winning garden designer known for her lush, romantic, densely-planted designs. Life with Flowers: Inspiration and Lessons from the Garden by Frances Palmer shares how her love of flowers feeds her art, with tips on choosing the best varieties for flower arranging plus recipes using flowers from the garden. How to Design a Garden by Pollyanna Wilkinson is a no-nonsense, hands-on, step-by-step guide to designing, making and managing a garden for new garden owners. Gardens Illustrated: The New Beautiful takes an expert look at 52 outstanding gardens created by some of the world's most talented garden designers with emphasis on sustainability. What Grows Together by Jamie Butterworth shares more than 60 tried-and-tested recipes for winning plant partnerships designed for different growing conditions. The Contemporary Garden by Phaidon examines 300 of the world's most innovative modern gardens across more than 40 countries. The Kindest Garden by Marian Boswall draws on her extensive experience as a professional landscape architect with expert knowledge of sustainable garden design, winner of this year's Garden Media Guild Gardening Book of the Year award. The Permaculture Garden by Huw Richards shares practical tips on how to create a healthy, productive, biodiverse garden using permaculture techniques.
with: The hugely respected British writer, columnist, lecturer and designer Mary Keen has created gardens all over the world as well as written about them, but it's the story of a year's turning in her own plot in Somerset that's the subject of her latest book.

A plants-person to her core, in her latest book she offers readers a fascinating and instructive insight into her work, breaking each photograph down into an explanation of the particular challenges of the site as well her expert analysis of how the design and planting overcame them.
Often described as the Vogue of the gardening world, the UK's Gardens Illustrated has forged a reputation as one of the world's most beautiful gardening magazines as well as its most inspiring.

Creating pleasing, enduring plant combinations is an art form that all creative gardeners strive to master, requiring an in-depth knowledge of plants' individual characteristics and growing preferences as well as a cultivated eye for beauty.
Serving as powerful testimony to the endlessly creative spirit of contemporary garden design, this is a book to feast on.

items.alt: From the no-dig approach to recipes using flowers from the garden, these books offer a range of tips and green perspectives. Photograph: Getty Images
